Category_Definition: Feedback about AT&T company policies contracts terms of service business practices or decisions that impact the customer experience — distinct from pricing or product quality.
Topic_Definition: Customer expresses intent to cancel service or switch to a competitor — with or without a stated reason.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer states they are leaving or have left AT&T without specifying a detailed reason. Include: 'cancel my account' 'I want to cancel' 'I'm leaving AT&T' 'im leaving att' 'I am dropping AT&T' 'I am cancelling ATT' 'I cancelled service' 'done with AT&T' 'leaving this company'. Primary churn signal — flag for retention outreach.

Category_Definition: Feedback about AT&T company policies contracts terms of service business practices or decisions that impact the customer experience — distinct from pricing or product quality.
Topic_Definition: Customer expresses intent to cancel service or switch to a competitor — with or without a stated reason.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer names Verizon specifically as the competitor they are switching to. Include: 'going to Verizon' 'switching to Verizon' 'Verizon is better' 'Verizon offers more' 'going to Verison'. Track separately for competitive intelligence — Verizon is primary wireless competitor.

Category_Definition: Feedback about AT&T company policies contracts terms of service business practices or decisions that impact the customer experience — distinct from pricing or product quality.
Topic_Definition: Customer expresses intent to cancel service or switch to a competitor — with or without a stated reason.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer names T-Mobile specifically as the competitor they are switching to. Include: 'going to T-Mobile' 'switching to T-Mobile' 'T-Mobile is cheaper' 'I'm going to T-Mobile' 'T-Mobile has better deals'. Track separately — T-Mobile is primary value/price competitor.

Category_Definition: Feedback about AT&T company policies contracts terms of service business practices or decisions that impact the customer experience — distinct from pricing or product quality.
Topic_Definition: Customer expresses intent to cancel service or switch to a competitor — with or without a stated reason.

Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer names a cable/ISP competitor for internet service. Include: 'going to Xfinity' 'switching to Spectrum' 'Comcast is cheaper' 'going back to cable' 'Google Fiber' 'Frontier has better speeds'. Track for broadband competitive intelligence.

Category_Definition: Feedback about AT&T company policies contracts terms of service business practices or decisions that impact the customer experience — distinct from pricing or product quality.

Include: 'too expensive so I'm leaving' 'can get it cheaper elsewhere' 'leaving because of the price' 'cost is why I'm switching' 'can't justify the bill'. Category_Definition: Feedback about AT&T company policies contracts terms of service business practices or decisions that impact the customer experience — distinct from pricing or product quality.
Topic_Definition: Customer expresses intent to cancel service or switch to a competitor — with or without a stated reason.

Include: 'leaving because service is terrible' 'coverage is why I'm leaving' 'switching because of all the outages' 'can't deal with dropped calls anymore' 'service is the reason I'm going'. Root cause signal for churn prevention.

Category_Definition: Feedback about AT&T company policies contracts terms of service business practices or decisions that impact the customer experience — distinct from pricing or product quality.
Topic_Definition: Customer expresses intent to cancel service or switch to a competitor — with or without a stated reason.

Include: 'leaving because of how I was treated' 'service experience is why I'm done' 'customer service drove me away' 'terrible support is why I'm switching' 'if this is how you treat customers I'm leaving'. Category_Definition: Feedback about AT&T company policies contracts terms of service business practices or decisions that impact the customer experience — distinct from pricing or product quality.
Topic_Definition: Customer expresses intent to cancel service or switch to a competitor — with or without a stated reason.

Include: 'was going to leave but they kept me' 'glad I stayed' 'offer convinced me to stay' 'rep saved my account' 'was about to cancel but they made it right'. Retention success signal — track what worked.

Category_Definition: Feedback about AT&T company policies contracts terms of service business practices or decisions that impact the customer experience — distinct from pricing or product quality.
Topic_Definition: Feedback about specific policies terms contracts or business practices the customer finds unfair or confusing.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer complains about contract terms length or conditions they find unfair. Include: 'your policies ****' 'lying in contract' 'honor your contract' 'your policies' 'bad policies' 'terms are unfair' 'locked in' 'contract is ridiculous' 'trapped in a contract' 'didn't know about the 2-year commitment'. Tag for policy review.

Category_Definition: Feedback about AT&T company policies contracts terms of service business practices or decisions that impact the customer experience — distinct from pricing or product quality.
Topic_Definition: Feedback about specific policies terms contracts or business practices the customer finds unfair or confusing.
Sub_Topic: Early Termination Fee / Cancellation Fee Complaint
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer complains about being charged to cancel or leave — ETF or cancellation penalty. Include: 'early termination fee' 'charged me to cancel' 'fee to leave' 'can't cancel without paying' 'penalized for leaving' 'exit fee' 'breakup fee'. Policy friction — competitive disadvantage if competitors don't charge.

Category_Definition: Feedback about AT&T company policies contracts terms of service business practices or decisions that impact the customer experience — distinct from pricing or product quality.

Category_Definition: Feedback about AT&T company policies contracts terms of service business practices or decisions that impact the customer experience — distinct from pricing or product quality.

Category_Definition: Feedback about AT&T company policies contracts terms of service business practices or decisions that impact the customer experience — distinct from pricing or product quality.

Category_Definition: Feedback about AT&T company policies contracts terms of service business practices or decisions that impact the customer experience — distinct from pricing or product quality.

Include: 'can't upgrade yet' 'not eligible' 'why can't I upgrade' 'have to wait too long to upgrade' 'upgrade policy is unfair' 'stuck with this phone' 'need to pay off to upgrade'. Category_Definition: Feedback about AT&T company policies contracts terms of service business practices or decisions that impact the customer experience — distinct from pricing or product quality.
Topic_Definition: Customer feedback about upgrade eligibility rules trade-in program terms or device upgrade policies.

Include: 'wouldn't accept my trade' 'phone had a scratch so no trade' 'trade-in requirements are too strict' 'device didn't qualify' 'rejected my trade-in' 'crack disqualified me'. Category_Definition: Feedback about AT&T company policies contracts terms of service business practices or decisions that impact the customer experience — distinct from pricing or product quality.

Category_Definition: Feedback about AT&T company policies contracts terms of service business practices or decisions that impact the customer experience — distinct from pricing or product quality.

Category_Definition: Feedback about AT&T company policies contracts terms of service business practices or decisions that impact the customer experience — distinct from pricing or product quality.

Category_Definition: Feedback about AT&T company policies contracts terms of service business practices or decisions that impact the customer experience — distinct from pricing or product quality.
Topic_Definition: Customer feedback about AT&T's use of outsourced foreign or non-domestic support staff as a policy concern.
Sub_Topic: Request to Stop Outsourcing / Use Local Staff
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer requests local or American agents as a company policy recommendation. Include: 'don't outsource' 'use local people' 'get some Americans' 'quit outsourcing' 'get correct answers' 'use local staff' 'bring jobs back to America' 'stop using overseas agents'. Workforce policy signal — distinct from communication barrier (People) by being a policy stance rather than personal experience.
